hero.title: Solar Panel Cleaning Robot Manufacturer in India
hero.subtitle: Engineers of a sustainable future — designing and building autonomous solar operations from Chakan, Pune.
hero.bodyBeforeLink: Taypro Private Limited is a Made-in-India manufacturer of autonomous and semi-automatic
hero.bodyAfterLink: for utility-scale, commercial, and rooftop PV. From our manufacturing hub in Chakan, Pune, we ship waterless dual-pass systems, pan-India spare logistics, and the NECTYR fleet dashboard, so developers and O&M teams can protect yield, safety, and water without compromising uptime.

statsStrip.heading: Manufacturing scale and field footprint
statsStrip.co2ReducedAnnually: Metric Tons Of CO2 Emission Reduced Annually

sustainability.body: Across 5+ GW of Taypro-supported capacity, waterless robotic cleaning avoids roughly 875 million litres of wet-wash water each year (vs. ~20 traditional passes at 3–4 L per module), recovers on the order of 188 GWh of additional clean generation annually versus wet baselines, and cuts grid CO₂ by tens of thousands of tonnes per year. See how operating teams quantify savings across live plants.
sustainability.co2ReducedLabel: Metric Tons Of CO2 Emission Reduced Annually
sustainability.communityHeading: Sustainable energy is our corporate responsibility and obligation to society.
sustainability.communityBody: We design for communities that depend on affordable power, and for the field teams who keep plants running through dust storms, monsoons, and peak summer loads.

investors.heading: Autonomous operations layer for utility-scale solar
investors.body: Taypro is building the health-monitoring and operations layer for India's utility solar fleet, using deployed cleaning robots as field data and execution infrastructure. Today: 5 GW+ capacity on NECTYR, 11 billion+ panel cleaning passes annually, 150+ live sites, recurring AMC relationships, and labelled cleaning telemetry since 2022. TAYPRO's AI model learns from every cleaning cycle across the active fleet — the operational dataset compounds with each site added and cannot be replicated by any competitor without equivalent field presence at utility scale. ORION plant intelligence is designed to attach at AMC renewal on rails we already operate.
investors.bullet0: Hardware + NECTYR are live across IPP and utility portfolios, with fleet operations teams use every day.
investors.bullet1: Self-learning fleet AI compounds a data moat competitors cannot replicate without utility-scale field presence — each clean refines soiling, timing, and routing models.
investors.bullet2: ORION early access is open for select asset owners under NDA, contact us for investor or strategic conversations.

story.heading: Company story, vision, and mission
story.intro: Taypro unifies robots, fleet software, and field service so solar asset owners get one accountable partner for autonomous operations, not disconnected hardware, crews, and spreadsheets.

whoWeAre.label: Who we are
whoWeAre.heading: The autonomous operations partner for utility-scale solar in India.
whoWeAre.body: Taypro Private Limited designs, manufactures, and supports waterless solar panel cleaning robots, and the NECTYR fleet layer that runs them at scale. From fixed tilt and trackers to Opex-managed fleets, we help IPPs and O&M teams recover generation, cut water and labour risk, and build a closed loop from field execution to plant outcomes.

vision.label: Vision
vision.heading: Unlock the full economic and environmental value of every solar megawatt.
vision.body: Soiling is one of the largest avoidable drags on generation. We believe renewable energy only scales when performance is dependable year after year. Taypro exists to give IPPs, developers, and O&M teams a repeatable way to protect yield, labour safety, and water resources on the same assets that power communities and industry.

mission.label: Mission
mission.heading: Ship reliable robots, transparent service, and measurable outcomes, site by site.
mission.body: We invest in R&D, manufacturing capacity, and nationwide logistics so customers receive consistent quality, rapid spare availability, and responsive support. From GLYDE and HELYX for distributed layouts to GLYDE-X for trackers, plus Taypro Opex for operator-led cleaning, our roadmap stays tied to one question: does it improve plant uptime, safety, and total cost of ownership?

solutions.heading: Robots, services, and fleet software
solutions.bodyBeforeLink: Taypro is a single vendor for hardware, operator-led cleaning, and cloud monitoring. Explore each platform for specifications, or read

robots.glyde: Fully autonomous, waterless dry cleaning with dual-pass airflow and microfiber, AI scheduling for fixed and seasonal-tilt utility plants.
robots.helyx: Portable pick-and-place dry cleaning robot for scattered blocks and plants that need flexible crew-assisted coverage.
robots.glydeX: Autonomous waterless robot for single-axis trackers, flexible bridge and 360° rotation across tracker brands.
robots.tayproOpex: Pay-per-panel-cleaned Opex, Taypro operates the cleaning robot fleet on your plant with no upfront CAPEX.
robots.nectyr: Fleet portal to schedule, monitor, and report on solar panel cleaning robots across your site.

builtForSites.heading: Built for real-world solar sites
builtForSites.paragraph1: Years of co-development with plant operators taught us that "works in a lab" is not enough. Taypro robots are engineered for uneven terrain, tracker curvature, RF-dark pockets, and the pace of utility-scale O&M, where every hour of downtime matters.
builtForSites.paragraph2BeforeGlyde: Patented dual-pass cleaning, AI-assisted scheduling, LTE/Wi-Fi/mesh connectivity, and predictive maintenance workflows come together in platforms such as
builtForSites.paragraph2After: , giving asset managers measurable control over soiling loss.

partnerJourney.heading: From first conversation to fleet operations
partnerJourney.intro: Developers, IPPs, and O&M teams typically follow a structured path with Taypro, whether you are evaluating a single block or a multi-hundred-megawatt portfolio.

step0.title: Understand your plant
step0.description: Share layout (fixed tilt, rooftop, or trackers), DC capacity, soiling profile, and whether you prefer CAPEX ownership or Taypro Opex as a managed service.

step1.title: Technical fit and ROI
step1.description: We map GLYDE, HELYX, or GLYDE-X plus NECTYR monitoring to your blocks, align on cleaning frequency, and use ROI tools to quantify water and labour savings.

step2.title: Deploy and commission
step2.description: Robots are manufactured in Chakan, Pune and shipped through our warehouse network. Commissioning crews install, train your O&M team, and connect fleets to NECTYR.

step3.title: Operate with continuity
step3.description: Nationwide spares, predictive maintenance workflows, and same-day breakdown response keep fleets productive. Case studies under Projects document outcomes at utility scale.

scale.heading: Scale you can spec with confidence
scale.body: Benchmarks reflect our manufacturing investments, nationwide logistics footprint, and obsession with repeatable cleaning performance across India's diverse solar geographies.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subheading: Quick answers for partners evaluating Taypro as their solar cleaning robotics vendor.
faq.a0: Taypro Private Limited builds autonomous and semi-automatic solar panel cleaning robots for utility-scale and commercial solar plants in India, including GLYDE (autonomous waterless cleaning), HELYX (portable dry cleaning), GLYDE-X for single-axis trackers, Taypro Opex as an operator-led cleaning service, and NECTYR for fleet monitoring and scheduling.
faq.q1: Where is Taypro based and where do you operate?
faq.a1: Taypro is headquartered and manufactures from Chakan, Pune, Maharashtra, with a warehouse network across India to support spare parts, commissioning, and same-day breakdown response for deployed robots.
faq.q2: Why is waterless robotic cleaning important for solar plants?
faq.a2: Water scarcity and logistics make manual or wet washing expensive and inconsistent. Taypro's patented dual-pass method first clears dry dust, then finishes with microfiber contact, helping plants save millions of litres annually while stabilising performance ratio through repeatable, weather-aware cleaning cycles.
faq.q3: How can developers or O&M teams evaluate Taypro?
faq.a3: Start with your plant layout (fixed tilt, rooftop, or trackers), capacity, and cleaning goals. Explore the solar panel cleaning system pages for technical specifications, case studies under Projects, or contact Taypro for a site-specific discussion, ROI inputs, and deployment planning.
faq.q4: Which Taypro robot fits fixed-tilt vs single-axis tracker plants?
faq.a4: GLYDE targets autonomous waterless cleaning on fixed and seasonal-tilt arrays. HELYX is portable for scattered blocks. GLYDE-X is engineered for single-axis trackers with a flexible bridge. Many sites combine models by block, our team maps robots to each array during site assessment.
faq.q5: Can we buy robots (CAPEX) or use an operator-led service (Opex)?
faq.a5: Both. CAPEX customers purchase robots with Taypro commissioning and spare support. Taypro Opex delivers operator-led cleaning without upfront robot capital, useful when you want predictable O&M costs and Taypro accountability for uptime.
faq.q6: What role does NECTYR play after installation?
faq.a6: NECTYR is the cloud dashboard for scheduling cleaning cycles, monitoring robot health, reviewing connectivity, and auditing fleet performance across blocks, helping O&M leads document cleaning history for performance ratio discussions with asset owners.
